rag_rules: Create range assumptions or guarantees only when the requirement provides concrete bounds or exposed constants.
Relate outputs only to symbols visible in the contract scope; avoid implementation-only paths in type-level contracts.
generic_rules: Use only identifiers confirmed by model facts or visible-symbol lists; never invent names.
Prefer simple deterministic expressions; introduce a helper equation only when it improves clarity or reuse.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use standard AGREE clause forms with semicolons: assume/guarantee with quoted labels, and eq/const/assign with declared names and types where required.
Use AGREE operators and temporal forms correctly: and, or, not, =>, nested if-then-else, and initialized recurrence with -> and pre.
anti_patterns: Do not mismatch helper declaration types with their expressions; comparisons produce bool and numeric expressions must use compatible numeric types.
Do not reference AADL property names or unit tokens as AGREE variables unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants.
